doc_title: Food Service Guidelines in CDC-Owned or -Operated Dining and Vending Facilities

steps.text: Program Official develops a Procurement Request Package, including a Statement of Work that reflects the minimum Food Service Guidelines requirements.
Food Service Guidelines Subject Matter Experts assist in integrating requirements into the Procurement Request Package, RFP, and final contract or permit.
Contracting Officer processes the Procurement Request Package and takes acquisition actions on behalf of the U.S. Government.
Incorporate reporting plan requirements (including monthly sales data, operating statements, and tracking methods) into the RFP and subsequent approved contract or permit.
Contracting Officer Representative (COR) or Project Officer determines post-award adequacy of vendor performance and ensures compliance through periodic reviews and inspections.

preconditions.text: The procurement is for a new or renegotiated food service contract or permit in a CDC-owned or -operated dining or vending facility, and is not subject to Section 3.E exceptions (such as existing contracts, employee-sponsored events, or emergency responses).

constraints.text: Require the vendor to comply with food and nutrition standards (prepared foods, packaged foods, beverages) at the standard implementation level at minimum.
Require the vendor to implement at least one behavioral design standard at the innovative implementation level at minimum.
Require the vendor to comply with facility efficiency, environmental support, and community development standards at the standard implementation level at minimum.
Require the vendor to comply with food safety standards at the standard level and explicitly require adherence to all applicable state and/or local food safety regulations.
